arts.ttl: Burnley team news for Stoke City New Year's Day clash as Scott Parker freshens things up
arts.sum: Scott Parker has opted to freshen things up for Burnley’s New Year’s Day clash against Stoke City.

arts.ttl: ‘Last season was a challenge’: Alan Pace reflects on ‘highs and lows’ of Burnley’s 2024 in New Year’s message
arts.sum: Alan Pace has penned a New Year’s message to Burnley supporters ahead of their first game of 2025 against Stoke City.

arts.ttl: Scott Parker on Burnley's Stoke City test, home form, gruelling schedule and injury latest
arts.sum: In-form Burnley will be out to begin the New Year with a positive result back at Turf Moor after a gruelling festive period.

arts.ttl: New Stoke City manager live - Mark Robins appointment confirmed, reaction, first games - Stoke-on-Trent Live
arts.sum: Tom Upton: Take care of the man, best Coventry manager I’ve had the pleasure of witnessing in my lifetime, worked absolute wonders for us, give him time and you’ll shoot up the table, just go easy on us in March when you come to the CBS. Franky: Good luck to Mark Robins wishing him all the best in his new role at Stoke from the SBA I hope he does well apart from when we play Stoke

arts.ttl: Stoke City sent clear message as surprise Mark Robins support emerges
arts.sum: There was widespread shock when Robins was sacked by Coventry in November after nearly eight years in charge, during which time he led the club to two promotions, the Championship play-off final and the FA Cup semi-finals. Stoke City have appointed Mark Robins as their new manager.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins appointed Stoke manager on three-and-a-half year deal
arts.sum: The former Coventry boss will take charge of his first game on Saturday against Plymouth Argyle

arts.ttl: Burnley player poised to extend loan spell away
arts.sum: Burnley winger Oluwaseun Adewumi is poised to stay at Dundee until the end of the season, their boss… The post Burnley player poised to extend loan spell away appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Stoke appoint Mark Robins as third manager of season on contract to 2028
arts.sum: Robins was sacked by Coventry in November He takes over from Narcís Pelach with club 18th Mark Robins has been appointed as Stoke’s manager on a three-and-a-half-year contract. The 55-year-old was sacked in November by Coventry and will watch Stoke’s game at Burnley on Wednesday from the stands before being in the dugout for the first time at home to Plymouth on Saturday. Robins spent just over seven and a half years at Coventry and oversaw two promotions to reach the Championship. His team lost the 2022-23 Championship playoff final on penalties to Luton and were beaten in a shootout by Manchester United in last season’s FA Cup semi-finals. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Stoke City appoint Mark Robins as new manager and ex-Cardiff City coach as assistant
arts.sum: Stoke City have confirmed the appointment of former Coventry City manager Mark Robins as their new boss on a three-and-a-half-year contract

arts.ttl: Stoke appoint ex-Coventry boss Robins as manager
arts.sum: Stoke City appoint former Coventry boss Mark Robins as their new manager on a three-and-a-half-year deal.
